title: Object 3
wikitext:
{{Rewrite In Progress|author=Homie Robot|rewriter=Avian|reason=Object 3 canon real!! Fell out of current standards, and the overall page could be fleshed out further. |date=June 16th, 2026}}{{DISPLAYTITLE:Object 3: "Nuclear Infection"}} ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Verdbears.png|caption='''Object 3''' under a microscope. The organisim featured is a verdbear.}} '''Object 3''' is a green, highly corrosive, and toxic substance found in very specific levels. Attempts to store the liquid in jars or containers are usually infeasible, as the substance dissolves a vast majority of materials. The most defining trait of '''Object 3''' is its ability to "infect" levels. Once exposed to one, it'll spread outwards by decaying the level's structure at a slow, but persistent rate, and eventually transform the entire space into a warped, infected mass. With nothing to obstruct it, the infection will eventually seep all throughout the level with ease. The most extreme case of the infection is [[Level 630]]—being the source of the substance. As a result, any entrances or exits leading to or from [[Level 630]], or any infected level in general, effectively become vulnerable points for '''Object 3''' to bleed through. Additional infected levels include [[Level 22]] and [[Level 64]], both compromised by sharing an entrance or exit with [[Level 630]]. Levels not having a direct entrance or exit to an infected level cannot be infected, unless if the substance is manually moved to another level. ==Usage and Location== Unlike various other objects, '''Object 3''' does not have a positive use whatsoever. It dissolves foreign substances (except for natural material, like dirt—which would still befall infection) on touch, including entities and wanderers. Exposure to the fumes provides various complication, the worst of which being organ failure and rapid development of cancer and other diseases. It is located on various levels, the most prominent of which being [[Level 22]] and [[Level 630]]. A less notable, yet prominent example is '''[[Level 64]]'''. [[Level 510|'''Level 510''']] has paintings drawn with a beta variant nuclear liquid ink, amount of this is also found in warehouses. It is rumoured that a mysterious entity known as '''"The Nucleigo"''' is the main cause of these infections. ==Verdbears== '''Verdbears''' are a type of airborne bacterium capable of surviving in environments contaminated with Nuclear Gas. Transmission occurs primarily through inhalation of the gas, allowing the bacteria to enter the upper respiratory tract. Upon exposure, Verdbears begin to reproduce in pulmonary tissues. The bacterium demonstrates rapid adaptability and may contribute to systemic infection, particularly in individuals already compromised by '''Object 3'''. ==Variant and Properties Chart== {| class="fandom-table" !Variant !Key Trait !Spread Method !Main Impact !Containment |- |'''Zero''' |Non reactive |No spread |Nil |Nil |- |'''Alpha''' |Rapid, aggressive decay |Direct contact, air |Mutant Entities |Nil |- |'''Beta''' |Subtle and slow spread |Air, water, cracks |Infects wanderers over time |Filtration |- |'''Gamma''' |Entity mutation |Direct contact |Nil |High-powered neutralization |} ==Recovered Note==
Me and James was already here, in this place when I woke up. We walked a while, without a clue, a goal. James saw life, trees with orange leaves. We were thirsty. I wandered again, looking for fresh water or a can of almond water, to quench our thirst. I sat down beneath the shade of a tree, my eyes sleepy and tired. James told me about a blue ocean he saw in a distance. It could be a hallucination, but I thought it was worth going there. As we reached closer, the blue ocean began to turn green. Only when I was so near, that I understood that this was never blue, it was never an ocean. It was a massive body of green liquid. I felt like taking a sip, but I didn't. I went further than James, to look for something edible. I heard James say "Hey, are you behind?". "No, I am over here. Can't you see me?", I yelled. "So, who is behind me?". Before I said, there was no one, he fell to that green acid. His scream, I still hear, each time I think of him, all the memories we had. From the group of 8 wanderers, only I... I was left alone, to see the deaths of my best friends. One killed the other, and he killed himself, one died of hunger, two others killed by thirst. One suffocated by the layers of sand, and another by this green acid. None was killed by the entites we saw. God left me alone. Why?... why?
This note was originally written in French. {{Collapse|num=1|initial='''> Open Author Information'''|final='''> Close Author Information'''|body='
